texto: That was as rough a thing as I ever heard tell of happening to a boy. And I'm mighty proud to learn how my boy stood up to it. You couldn't ask any more of a grown man... It's not a thing you can forget. I don't guess it's a thing you ought to forget. What I mean is, things like that happen. They may seem mighty cruel and unfair, but that's how life is part of the time. But that isn't the only way life is. A part of the time, it's mighty good. And a man can't afford to waste all the good part, worrying about the bad parts. That makes it all bad.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Fred Gipson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “That was as rough a thing as I ever heard tell of happening to a boy. And I'm mighty proud to lea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, gratitude, manhood pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de manhood e maturity.

frase_original: That was as rough a thing as I ever heard tell of happening to a boy. And I'm mighty proud to learn how my boy stood up to it. You couldn't ask any more of a grown man... It's not a thing you can forget. I don't guess it's a thing you ought to forget. What I mean is, things like that happen. They may seem mighty cruel and unfair, but that's how life is part of the time. But that isn't the only way life is. A part of the time, it's mighty good. And a man can't afford to waste all the good part, worrying about the bad parts. That makes it all bad.

texto: Well, when you're fourteen years old, you can't afford to mix in a rock fight with your five-year-old brother. You can't do it, even when you're in the right. You just can't explain a thing like that to your folks. All they'll do is point out how much bigger you are, how unfair it is to your little brother.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Fred Gipson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Well, when you're fourteen years old, you can't afford to mix in a rock fight with your five-year…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, battles, immaturity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de immaturity e maturity.

frase_original: Well, when you're fourteen years old, you can't afford to mix in a rock fight with your five-year-old brother. You can't do it, even when you're in the right. You just can't explain a thing like that to your folks. All they'll do is point out how much bigger you are, how unfair it is to your little brother.

texto: ... I guessed that when you are nearly a man, you have to learn to put up with a lot of aggravation from little old bitty kids.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Fred Gipson sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. A afirmação “... I guessed that when you are nearly a man, you have to learn to put up with a lot of aggravati…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, growing-up, immaturity, útil em momentos de immaturity e maturity.
